meta.scoring: Start at 100, subtract: first_party_modifier(0-75), velocity_penalty(velocity%*0.3), saturation_penalty(repos*3 max 30), top_n_penalty(20 if exists). Floor 0.

competitors_found.name: Anthropic Claude Agent SDK (auto-compact + context editing)
competitors_found.key_metric: Built-in compaction and context editing that auto-manages token budget in agent loops

competitors_found.name: OpenAI Agents SDK (session trimming + usage tracking)
competitors_found.key_metric: Built-in session memory with trimming/compression; aggregated token usage tracking per run

competitors_found.name: LangChain Context Engineering Module
competitors_found.key_metric: Progressive disclosure, compression, routing, tool management patterns; dedicated context docs

competitors_found.name: MCP (Model Context Protocol)
competitors_found.key_metric: Industry standard (Linux Foundation); progressive discovery reduces tool token usage by 98.7%

competitors_found.name: know-cli (Token Budget Allocator)
competitors_found.key_metric: PyPI package with --context-budget flag; allocates 60% code, 15% imports, 15% summaries, 10% overview

competitors_found.name: Manus Context Engine
competitors_found.key_metric: Production context engineering with documented token allocation strategies

competitors_found.name: Context-Engineering-for-Multi-Agent-Systems (GitHub)
competitors_found.key_metric: Universal Context Engine with token & cost analytics; updated March 2026

competitors_found.name: Agent-Skills-for-Context-Engineering (GitHub)
competitors_found.key_metric: Comprehensive agent skills collection for context management in production

reality_checks.first_party_details: Both Anthropic (auto-compact, context editing) and OpenAI (session trimming, usage tracking) have GA built-in context management. Claude Agent SDK auto-summarizes at 95% capacity. LangChain has dedicated context engineering docs.
reality_checks.velocity_details: MCP adopted by all major providers and donated to Linux Foundation. Anthropic shipped context editing in late 2025. Multiple context engineering blog posts and tools in Q1 2026. 'State of Context Engineering in 2026' articles exist.

scoring_breakdown.first_party_rationale: Both Anthropic AND OpenAI have GA solutions. Claude Agent SDK auto-compact is essentially a built-in context budget manager. MCP progressive discovery handles tool context allocation.
scoring_breakdown.velocity_rationale: ~80% of the problem space addressed in last 6 months; 80*0.3=24
scoring_breakdown.top_n_rationale: No specific 'top N context budget managers' list found

reality_checks.differentiation_angle: The first-party solutions from Anthropic and OpenAI directly address this. Claude Agent SDK's auto-compact is literally a unified context budget manager. MCP's progressive discovery handles tool context allocation. LangChain provides the middleware layer. The only remaining niche might be cross-provider context budget orchestration (managing budgets across Claude + GPT + Gemini in a single workflow), but this is an extremely narrow use case.

competitors_found.name: Browserbase Stagehand (Live View + action preview)
competitors_found.key_metric: Live View iFrame for real-time monitoring; action preview before execution; human browser control takeover

competitors_found.name: OpenAI Agents SDK (human-in-the-loop)
competitors_found.key_metric: Built-in approval-based HITL with RunToolApprovalItem; pause/resume/approve/reject workflow

competitors_found.name: LangGraph (interrupt() function)
competitors_found.key_metric: interrupt() pauses graph mid-execution for human input; structured approval workflows

competitors_found.name: Browser Use (agent-sdk)
competitors_found.key_metric: 81k+ GitHub stars; HITL as feature request/implementation; 89.1% WebVoyager benchmark

competitors_found.name: Cloudflare Agents (human-in-the-loop)
competitors_found.key_metric: Dedicated HITL docs for agent workflows

competitors_found.name: Vercel AI SDK 6
competitors_found.key_metric: Tool execution approval for HITL control; improved in SDK 6

competitors_found.name: AgentScope
competitors_found.key_metric: Built-in ReAct agent with HITL steering, memory, planning

competitors_found.name: Permit.io HITL for AI Agents
competitors_found.key_metric: Best practices framework for HITL in agent workflows

reality_checks.first_party_details: OpenAI Agents SDK has full approval-based HITL with pause/resume. Vercel AI SDK 6 has tool execution approval. Cloudflare has dedicated HITL docs. These are first-party solutions from major platforms.
reality_checks.velocity_details: HITL has become a standard security feature across all browser agent frameworks. Multiple 'Top 10 Browser Agents 2026' lists exist. Stagehand Live View, OpenAI HITL, LangGraph interrupt() all shipped/matured in last 6 months.
reality_checks.top_n_details: Multiple 'top N browser agent' lists exist: '11 Best AI Browser Agents in 2026' (Firecrawl), '10 Best Agentic Browsers' (BrightData), 'Top 10 Browser AI Agents 2026' (o-mega), 'Best 30+ Open Source Web Agents' (aimultiple)

scoring_breakdown.first_party_rationale: OpenAI, Vercel, Cloudflare all have GA HITL. Stagehand has Live View takeover. However, no single tool combines failure detection + auto-escalation + approval in one browser-specific SDK.
scoring_breakdown.top_n_rationale: Multiple 'top N browser agent' comparison articles exist

reality_checks.differentiation_angle: The HITL pattern is now table-stakes across all agent frameworks. Stagehand's Live View provides browser-specific human takeover. OpenAI's approval workflow handles pause/resume. LangGraph's interrupt() handles arbitrary human checkpoints. The thesis that 'no SDK provides failure detection + human takeover + approval workflows' is factually incorrect as of 2026. The space is saturated with solutions.

competitors_found.name: HuggingFace Optimum
competitors_found.key_metric: Unified API for ONNX export, quantization, graph optimization; supports ONNX Runtime, OpenVINO, ExecuTorch backends

competitors_found.name: llama.cpp convert/quantize scripts
competitors_found.key_metric: HF-to-GGUF conversion + quantization (2-8 bit); gguf-my-repo one-click HuggingFace tool

competitors_found.name: NVIDIA TensorRT / TensorRT Edge-LLM
competitors_found.key_metric: HF checkpoint to ONNX to TensorRT pipeline; Edge-LLM for Jetson/DRIVE platforms

competitors_found.name: RunAnywhere
competitors_found.key_metric: Modular SDKs supporting GGUF, ONNX, CoreML/MLX backends; OTA model updates; enterprise control plane

competitors_found.name: Apple coremltools
competitors_found.key_metric: Converts TorchScript, SavedModel, ONNX, Keras to .mlmodel

competitors_found.name: Intel OpenVINO
competitors_found.key_metric: Model optimization + quantization for Intel hardware; integrated with HF Optimum

competitors_found.name: UniQL (Unified Quantization + Low-rank)
competitors_found.key_metric: One-shot cloud quantization with on-device configurable pruning; supports Transformers, SSMs, hybrid models

reality_checks.first_party_details: HuggingFace Optimum is essentially the unified conversion hub. NVIDIA provides TensorRT conversion. Apple provides coremltools. Intel provides OpenVINO. Each major hardware vendor has their own conversion tool, and HF Optimum integrates several.
reality_checks.velocity_details: NVIDIA shipped TensorRT Edge-LLM in 2025-2026. RunAnywhere emerged as a multi-format platform. HuggingFace gguf-my-repo simplifies GGUF conversion. UniQL published. Multiple 'choosing the right format' guides published in 2026.
reality_checks.top_n_details: 'Top Edge AI Solutions in 2026' (RunAnywhere), 'Top 10 Edge AI Frameworks for 2025', comparison guides on GGUF vs GPTQ vs AWQ

scoring_breakdown.first_party_rationale: HuggingFace Optimum is the de facto hub. Each vendor (NVIDIA, Apple, Intel) has GA conversion tools. RunAnywhere consolidates multi-format. But no single tool does ALL formats with auto-quantization in one click.
scoring_breakdown.top_n_rationale: Multiple comparison articles and 'top N' edge AI lists exist

reality_checks.differentiation_angle: HuggingFace Optimum already serves as the conversion hub for most formats. The remaining gap (a true 'one-click convert to ALL formats simultaneously') is technically possible but commercially weak: developers typically target 1-2 deployment formats, not all simultaneously. RunAnywhere already offers multi-format SDK support. The value proposition is too narrow against entrenched vendor tools.

reality_checks.gap_title: Unified Function Calling Data Format & Converter

competitors_found.name: xLAM (Salesforce) - standardized trajectory format
competitors_found.key_metric: Aggregates agent trajectories from distinct environments into consistent format; #1 on Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard

competitors_found.name: Gorilla / BFCL (Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard)
competitors_found.key_metric: BFCL V4 Agentic with standardized evaluation; format sensitivity evaluation

competitors_found.name: ToolMind unified dataset
competitors_found.key_metric: Unifies xlam-60k, When2Call, glaive-v2, ToolACE, BUTTONInstruct, APIGen-MT-5k, Tau-bench into single format

competitors_found.name: TRL (HuggingFace) tool calling training support
competitors_found.key_metric: Native fine-tuning support for custom tools since trl 0.19; standardized conversation format with tool_calls field

competitors_found.name: LLaMA-Factory format conversion (Alpaca/ShareGPT/ChatML)
competitors_found.key_metric: standardize_sharegpt function; supports Alpaca, ShareGPT, ChatML formats with field mapping

competitors_found.name: Unsloth datasets guide
competitors_found.key_metric: Comprehensive dataset format guide with conversion utilities for function calling training

competitors_found.name: Granite-FunctionCalling (IBM)
competitors_found.key_metric: Repurposes datasets by re-instructing for function calling and unifying output schema to structured JSON

competitors_found.name: Unified Tool Integration Protocol (arxiv 2508.02979)
competitors_found.key_metric: Protocol-agnostic approach to function calling across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google formats

reality_checks.first_party_details: HuggingFace TRL has native tool calling training support since v0.19. xLAM provides standardized format. ToolMind already unifies 7+ datasets. MCP provides a standard tool interface protocol. LLaMA-Factory handles format conversion.
reality_checks.velocity_details: TRL v0.19+ added native tool calling support. ToolMind unified 7 datasets. xLAM hit #1 on BFCL. BFCL V4 Agentic released. Unified Tool Integration paper published. ~70% of problem solved in last 6 months.

scoring_breakdown.first_party_rationale: HuggingFace TRL has native support. xLAM standardizes formats. ToolMind unifies datasets. But no single 'universal converter CLI' exists that auto-converts between all formats bidirectionally.
scoring_breakdown.top_n_rationale: No 'top N function calling data converters' list found

reality_checks.differentiation_angle: The thesis is largely disproven. TRL handles training format natively. xLAM and ToolMind have already unified major datasets. LLaMA-Factory converts between Alpaca/ShareGPT/ChatML. The remaining narrow gap -- a CLI tool that bidirectionally converts between ALL provider-specific function calling schemas -- is a utility, not a product. The academic paper on Unified Tool Integration shows the research community considers this largely solved at the protocol level via MCP.

competitors_found.name: TRL trl.rewards module (built-in reward functions)
competitors_found.key_metric: Built-in composable reward functions: accuracy_reward, reasoning_accuracy_reward, think_format_reward, get_soft_overlong_punishment; supports weighted sum of multiple rewards

competitors_found.name: rLLM framework
competitors_found.key_metric: Reward functions scoring results with multiple RL algorithms (GRPO, REINFORCE, RLOO, rejection sampling)

competitors_found.name: Eureka (NVIDIA)
competitors_found.key_metric: LLM-generated reward functions that outperform human-engineered rewards; evolutionary optimization over reward code

competitors_found.name: Text2Reward
competitors_found.key_metric: Automated dense reward function generation from natural language descriptions

competitors_found.name: RLHF-Reward-Modeling (RLHFlow)
competitors_found.key_metric: Recipes to train reward models for RLHF; Decision-Tree-Reward achieving 95.4% on RewardBench

competitors_found.name: awesome-RLVR (OpenDILab)
competitors_found.key_metric: Curated list of RLVR resources; continually updated

reality_checks.first_party_details: HuggingFace TRL v1.0 has a built-in trl.rewards module with composable reward functions and weighted sum support. GRPOTrainer natively supports multiple reward functions. This is essentially a mini reward function registry within the de facto standard RL training library.
reality_checks.velocity_details: TRL shipped trl.rewards module with composable functions. RLVR became a major paradigm. Eureka showed LLMs can auto-generate reward functions (potentially obsoleting a marketplace). RewardBench 2 released. ~50% of problem addressed in last 6 months.

scoring_breakdown.first_party_rationale: TRL has built-in composable rewards but only 4 functions. Not a full marketplace. However, Eureka's approach of LLM-generated rewards may make a marketplace unnecessary.
scoring_breakdown.top_n_rationale: No 'top N reward function' marketplace list found

reality_checks.differentiation_angle: TRL's trl.rewards module has only 4 built-in functions -- there IS room for a community-contributed registry of tested, domain-specific reward functions (code quality, safety, factuality, citation accuracy, SQL correctness, etc.). However, Eureka and Text2Reward show that LLMs can auto-generate reward functions, which undermines the marketplace model. The viable angle is a curated, benchmarked registry with standardized evaluation metrics (like RewardBench scores) for each reward function, integrated as a TRL plugin. Focus on RLVR verifiers for non-math/code domains where the 'verifier problem' remains unsolved.
